segments.text: SUSIE: Hello?
segments.translation: 苏西：你好？

segments.text: PAUL: Hi, Susie, it's Paul here.
segments.translation: 保罗：嗨，苏西，我是保罗。

segments.text: How are you? Enjoying your new job?
segments.translation: 最近怎么样？喜欢你的新工作吗？

segments.text: You're working at the library, aren't you?
segments.translation: 你是在图书馆工作，是吧？

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes. I started when the library re-opened a month ago. It's great.
segments.translation: 苏西：是的。一个月前图书馆重新开馆时我开始在这工作的。图书馆很棒。

segments.text: PAUL: Actually Carol and I have been meaning to join for a while.
segments.translation: 保罗：实际上卡洛和我想来这已经有一段时间了。

segments.text: SUSIE: Oh, you should. It doesn't cost anything, and the new library has all sorts of facilities.
segments.translation: 苏西：嗯，你们应该来的。不需要花钱，而且新图书馆有各种各样的设施。

segments.text: It's not Just a place where you borrow books.
segments.translation: 它不仅仅是一个借书的地方。

segments.text: For instance, there's an area with comfortable seats where you can sit and read the magazines they have there.
segments.translation: 比如说，馆内有一片区域有很舒服的位子可以坐，还可以看那儿的杂志。

segments.text: Some people spend the whole morning there.
segments.translation: 有的人整个早上都在那。

segments.text: PAUL: Mmm. Wish I had that amount of time to spend!
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯。真希望我也有那么多时间。

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes, you must be pretty busy at present, with the children and everything?
segments.translation: 苏西：哦，你现在一定很忙吧，忙着照顾孩子什么的。

segments.text: PAUL: We are, yes. But we're hoping to get away this summer.
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯，是的。不过我们希望这个夏天能出去度假。

segments.text: We're thinking of going to Greece.
segments.translation: 我们正考虑去希腊。

segments.text: SUSIE: Well, we've got a much larger section of the library devoted to travel books now, so you should come and have a look.
segments.translation: 苏西：嗯，我们图书馆用于陈列旅游书籍的部分比以前大了很多，你可以过来看看。

segments.text: I can't remember if there's anything specifically on Greece, but I should think so.
segments.translation: 我不记得有没有专门讲希腊的书，但我想应该有。

segments.text: PAUL: OK. Now Carol's organising a project for the history class she teaches at school - it's about life in the town a hundred years ago.
segments.translation: 保罗：好的。卡洛目前正在为她在学校教的历史课组织一个活动——是关于100年前这个城市的生活的。

segments.text: Do you have anything that might be useful?
segments.translation: 你那有没有什么有用的资料？

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes, actually we've now got a new section with materials on the history of the town and surrounding region.
segments.translation: 苏西：有的，事实上我们图书馆新开辟了一个部分，用于陈列这座城市及其周边区域的历史资料。

segments.text: PAUL: Right. I'll tell her.
segments.translation: 保罗：好的。我会告诉她的。

segments.text: You can't always find that sort of thing on the internet.
segments.translation: 你并一定总能在网上找到这些东西。

segments.text: Now in the old library there used to be a separate room with reference books.
segments.translation: 过去，在老图书馆里有一个单独的屋子存放参考书。

segments.text: It was a really nice quiet room.
segments.translation: 那真是一个又安静又好的地方。

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes. We've put those books in the main part of the library now, but we do have a room called the community room.
segments.translation: 苏西：是的。我们如今把那些书放在图书馆主馆，不过我们现在也有一个叫做社区用室的屋子。

segments.text: It can be hired out for meetings, but at other times people can use it to study.
segments.translation: 它可以被租出去用于开会，其他时候人们可以在里面学习。

segments.text: PAUL: I might use that.
segments.translation: 保罗：或许我会去那儿学习。

segments.text: It's hard to find anywhere quiet at home sometimes.
segments.translation: 有的时候想在家找到个安静的地方很难。

segments.text: SUSIE: I can't remember how old your son and daughter are ... we've introduced a special section of fiction written specially for teenagers, but they might be a bit young for that?
segments.translation: 苏西：我不记得你的儿子、女儿多大了……我们开辟了一个专为青少年写的小说的单元，但他们可能还是小了一点。

segments.text: PAUL: Yes, they would be.
segments.translation: 保罗：是的，他们还小。

segments.text: SUSIE: Well, we do have lots of activities for younger children.
segments.translation: 苏西：嗯，我们有很多针对年纪较小的孩子的活动。

segments.text: PAUL: Yes?
segments.translation: 保罗：是吗？

segments.text: SUSIE: For example we have a Science Club.
segments.translation: 苏西：比如说我们有一个“科学俱乐部”。

segments.text: At the next meeting, they're going to be doing experiments with stuff that everyone has in the kitchen - sugar and flour and so on.
segments.translation: 在俱乐部的下次会面上，孩子们会利用每家厨房里都有的东西——糖、面粉等做实验。

segments.text: PAUL: They might be interested, yes.
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯，我的孩子们也许会有兴趣。

segments.text: SUSIE: And we have a competition for children called Reading Challenge.
segments.translation: 苏西：我们还有一个比赛叫做“阅读挑战”。

segments.text: That doesn 't begin until after the end of term.
segments.translation: 这个比赛要到学期末过后才开始。

segments.text: They have to read six books, and they get a certificate if they manage it.
segments.translation: 他们要读完六本书，如果做到了的话就能得到一个证书。

segments.text: PAUL: So that gives them something to do while they're on holiday, instead of getting bored.
segments.translation: 保罗：这样他们假期就有事做而不会无聊了。

segments.text: SUSIE: That's the idea.
segments.translation: 苏西：嗯，就是这么想的。

segments.text: And there's special activities for adults too.
segments.translation: 图书馆也有针对成年人的特别活动。

segments.text: On Friday we have a local author called Tanya Streep who's going to be talking about her new novel.
segments.translation: 星期五，我们邀请了本地作家Tanya Streep来谈谈她的最新小说。

segments.text: It's called 'Catch the Mouse' and she based the story on a crime that actually took place here years ago.
segments.translation: 小说名叫《Catch the Mouse》，里面的故事是基于一个很多年前在这里发生的一起犯罪事件。

segments.text: PAUL: Right. We're not free on Friday, but I'll look out for the book.
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯，我们周五没空，不过我会找找这本小说。

segments.text: SUSIE: Now this probably isn't for you, but we do have IT support available for members.
segments.translation: 苏西：还有一个活动可能不适合你，针对会员我们推出了信息技术支持。

segments.text: We get quite a few older people coming along who are wanting to get up to speed with computer technology.
segments.translation: 有不少老人过来，想要跟上电脑技术的发展。

segments.text: It's on Tuesday mornings - they don't need to make an appointment or anything, they just turn up.
segments.translation: 这个活动是在星期二上午——他们不需要事先约定，到时候自然就来了。

segments.text: PAUL: Well , my mother might be interested, I'll let her know.
segments.translation: 保罗：嗯，我母亲或许会感兴趣，我会告诉她的。

segments.text: SUSIE: OK. And there's another service which you wouldn't expect from a library, which is a free medical check-up.
segments.translation: 苏西：好。还有一个活动你可能想不到图书馆会有，那就是免费的医疗检查。

segments.text: The hospital arranges for someone to come along and measure the level of sugar in your blood, and they check cholesterol levels at the same time.
segments.translation: 医院安排了人过来，可以测血糖，同时他们还会检测胆固醇水平。

segments.text: PAUL: Really?
segments.translation: 保罗：真的吗？

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes, but that's only for the over-60s, so you wouldn't qualify.
segments.translation: 苏西：是的，不过这项服务仅提供给60岁以上的人，所以你不能参与。

segments.text: PAUL: OK. Well, I'll tell my mother, she might be interested.
segments.translation: 保罗：好的。我会告诉我妈，她也许会有兴趣。

segments.text: SUSIE: What other information ... well , we do have a little shop with things like wallcharts and greetings cards, and also stamps so you can post the cards straightaway, which is really useful.
segments.translation: 苏西：至于别的什么信息嘛……哦，我们还有一个小商店卖挂图、贺卡之类的东西，还有邮票，很有用，你可以在那直接把卡片寄出去。

segments.text: PAUL: Yeah. Well, I'll bring the children round at the weekend and we'll join.
segments.translation: 保罗：好的。周末我会带孩子们过来，我们要加入进来。

segments.text: Oh, one more thing - I'll be bringing the car, is there parking available?
segments.translation: 哦，还有一件事——我会开车过来，图书馆有停车的地方吗？

segments.text: SUSIE: Yes, and it's free in the evening and at weekends.
segments.translation: 苏西：有的，而且在晚上和周末免费。

segments.text: PAUL: Perfect. Well, thanks, Susie see you soon …
segments.translation: 保罗：完美。好的，谢谢你，苏西，再见……
